events.label: Crew reduced 7 → 5
events.txt: Francois and Iwona scratch (sickness/injury). Decision was actually confirmed earlier than this — pinned to Friday race-morning here so it doesn’t sit off the left edge of the timeline.

events.label: Off the dock
events.txt: Departed for the start line. Final prep: dropped J1, raised and furled J3.

events.label: Warning signal
events.txt: Sequence 8 warning.

events.label: START
events.txt: Close-hauled beat in 20+ kts. Crew not yet in foulies — everyone soaked before the first watch change. Lesson logged early.

events.label: Foulies, one by one
events.txt: Heavily heeled, crew went below individually to suit up. Brad's stomach turned from the extended time in the cabin.

events.label: Seasickness sets in (mostly)
events.txt: Two of the five crew feel the sea state Friday evening and are sailing through it — on deck, on watch, getting the job done, just not at their sharpest. A third brief episode was logged as seasickness at the time but is corrected here for the record 😂 — it was an electrolyte drink mix with artificial sweeteners, which Sebastian's stomach doesn't tolerate. Nobody had pre-medicated. Both lessons stand — pre-race meds are cheap insurance, and the galley plan should vet electrolyte mixes for artificial ingredients.

events.label: Evening watch settles in
events.txt: Hot burritos from the galley; ~20 kts, holding J3. First trick on the wheel for the newest offshore hand.

events.label: All-hands: reef + Reacher
events.txt: 15-minute debate (Reacher vs Code 0 vs A3 vs reef) settles on reef + Reacher. Darius up the mast to help reef; Darius and Brad on the foredeck for the Reacher. Hoist goes poorly — confusion over which side the halyard is on, what sheet runs over what. Coordination by yelling and relay. No maneuver card existed — the crew never fully assembled pre-race to build one. Lesson logged at the time: reef earlier, don't rush midnight all-hands changes.

events.label: New watch — J3 furled
events.txt: Brad + Steve on; Darius stays up the whole shift. Briefly double-headed J3 + Reacher; main kept overpowering so J3 comes off. Still overpowered but manageable.

events.label: INSIGHT · The cost of chaos
events.txt: Tracker analysis: RAGANA bled roughly 90–130 minutes to her rating twin Christopher Dragon across Friday night and Saturday — the window containing the un-carded 01:45 all-hands change, reactive reefs, and a lost sheet. Against the eventual class winner Gesture, most of the final deficit accrued here, not in the light air.

events.label: Watch extended an hour
events.txt: Sebastian and Matt still unwell; Brad + Steve cover an extra hour before being relieved.

events.label: 2nd reef (late, reactive)
events.txt: Main had been eased so far the first spreader chafed a visible mark into it before the reef went in. Brad up most of the day managing broaches — vang and main releases.

events.label: Reacher sheet lost overboard
events.txt: No stopper knot on the sheet end — it ran. Forced a douse instead of a furl. 2nd reef + J3 finally finds a good balance.

events.label: Brad drives 10-ft waves
events.txt: Two hours on the wheel in the biggest sea state of the race.

events.label: GULF STREAM entry
events.txt: 38°10′N 68°44′W, 74°F, 2 reefs + J3, ~8 kts. Logged as 16:21 but Steve's watch was running UTC−5 instead of UTC−4 — true time confirmed as 17:21 EDT (0.7 nm match on the tracker).

events.label: Shook 2nd reef
events.txt: Brad on the wheel, Darius on the mast, Steve in the pit.

events.label: Freeze-dried dinner
events.txt: Chicken pesto pasta — too much water, tasted like chicken noodle soup. "Was ok."

events.label: Stream core, 80°F
events.txt: 9.7 kts logged at 22:42 — favorable current, wind behind, best speeds of the race.

events.label: Back on, driving 1.5 hrs
events.txt: Similar conditions to the evening watch.

events.label: BATTERY BLACKOUT
events.txt: House bank dead; full instrument outage ~10 min. Brad steering by the stars. Engine charge; daily charging thereafter, but no formal schedule.

events.label: A3 up
events.txt: Brad on the foredeck, coached by Steve on the pit; Sebastian driving, Matt assisting bow. Reminders needed on the spin-halyard untangle and the tackline set for the inside jibe.

events.label: South of the Stream
events.txt: 75°F at 36°51′N — crossing complete in ~8–12 hrs, on plan. Anticlimactic, i.e. nailed.

events.label: A3 down — big left shift
events.txt: Wind swings 300→210. The race turns into an upwind beat from here to Bermuda.

events.label: Recovery window
events.txt: A calm midday stretch — rest, drying gear, cleanup. Rest banked here mattered later (see the light-air doctrine).

events.label: INSIGHT · The Sunday beat paid
events.txt: From roughly 265 nm to 180 nm to go, RAGANA’s east lane gained ~20 minutes per 10 nm on Christopher Dragon’s west lane — continuously, for 80 nm — flipping a 94-minute deficit into a 75-minute lead by 22:13 Sunday. Upwind in 8–11 kts of flat water is this boat’s design sweet spot.

events.label: Satcomm: GRIBs yes, phone finicky
events.txt: The Iridium GO! pulled GRIB weather files for the navigators most of the time — but getting it working was painful and pulled Steve off other jobs more than once. On Brad’s phone, only one messaging app would connect. Worth drilling the whole satcomm workflow, and every app, before the start.

events.label: Wind dying
events.txt: The forecast “wall of little wind” south of 36°N arrives. Under 5 kts by late watch; Brad drives ~2 hrs. Still east of rhumb; EU model calls for a midnight tack.

events.label: Tack to port — back to rhumb
events.txt: Shift 200–240 → 140–170. Optimism aboard — mid-fleet among boats considered faster.

events.label: Leech tear noticed
events.txt: Upper leech of the mainsail found tearing. Steve suspects flogging plus reef-induced chafe.

events.label: SPEEDO FAILS
events.txt: Boatspeed reads zero or garbage — every derived true-wind number goes with it. 03:00: Brad strips the displays to AWA + SOG only. "Now we're dinghy sailing."

events.label: Stay with J3 over J1
events.txt: Considered the J1 for the light air; stuck with J3 — same size, rougher shape reasoned to hold better in the leftover chop.

events.label: INSIGHT · Everyone parked
events.txt: Fair-metric finding: measured over the same stretch of ocean (180→80 nm to go), every boat in the comparison set parked — 16–38% of each traversal under 4 kts. The spread between best and worst traversal was only 5.5 hours. RAGANA’s 27.9 h was second-slowest: an honest penalty of ~2–4 hours vs peers, from the most-east lane, with a speedo failure and a sail repair falling inside the dead core.

events.label: Mainsail leech patch
events.txt: ~1 hr repair, Steve driving under jib alone at 2.5 kts SOG. Steve has not slept to keep the boat moving.

events.label: J3 → J1
events.txt: Clean change, Brad on the foredeck. Light-air inventory call for the beat.

events.label: Breakfast + galley rhythm
events.txt: Hot breakfast; dishes done with a seawater bucket — the routine smoothing out by day four.

events.label: Dolphins + birds
events.txt: So many of them, with birds working the same water.

events.label: BSP returns
events.txt: Display fallback profiles built, including a no-BSP backup, plus helm hot-keys.

events.label: Humpback dead ahead
events.txt: Near-miss — could have hit it. Wind building.

events.label: Three-way helm split
events.txt: 130 nm to go, Bermuda just below layline. VMG (Sebastian) vs boat speed (Darius) vs layline (Steve) — helm rotates, each sails their own idea. Fleet visibly sailing higher.

events.label: Steve takes the layline
events.txt: Steve back on the helm after rest, commits to the layline. Faster-looking boats cross to windward.

events.label: INSIGHT · Position × timing
events.txt: Within the cohort entering the light-air zone the same evening, boats nearer the rhumb line traversed it 1.5–4 hours faster (Carina: same entry hour, ~10 nm further west, 3.7 h quicker). Yet the overall podium — Nicole, Selkie, Towhee — won from the east: as lower-rated boats they arrived 3–5 hours later, when the Monday-evening rebuild was closer. Lane choice and arrival timing both mattered.

events.label: Parked, 3 kts — tack to port
events.txt: Sebastian tacks; Steve disagrees, wants to hold near rhumb. 20:40: an unexpected SE'ly (150°T) straight from Bermuda arrives — the tack pays a little, against the forecast.

events.label: Fog, rebuild, an hour on the wheel
events.txt: Wind rebuilds 6→11+ kts; confused chop, thick fog, no visibility beyond nearby boats, very humid. "Like driving a race car" — stable enough to hold with one hand at times.

events.label: Shift to 210 — layline made
events.txt: "Right on the money for a finish on the layline."

events.label: INSIGHT · The near-catch
events.txt: RAGANA closed Hissy Fit II (J/120) from 159 minutes behind at 400 nm to go to just 9 minutes at 20 nm to go — and lost to her by 2m15s corrected. The back half of this race was sailed well.

events.label: Finish prep begins
events.txt: Very shifty conditions; Steve navigating multiple tacks into the approach.

events.label: The wind cloud
events.txt: Missed going under a wind cloud in the final hours — cost a few miles, ~28th → 39th provisional.

events.label: FINISH
events.txt: 15:54:52 EDT. Elapsed 4d 01:34:52. 46th/86 SDL Overall, 8th/10 SDL 3 — up from 71st/99 in 2022.

recon.note: Log dated "Sat" — race started Fri 14:20, this is Friday afternoon.
Same date mislabel as above — Friday evening, not Saturday.

CONFIRMED — Steve's watch (and some boat instruments) were running UTC−5 (EST) instead of UTC−4 (EDT), i.e. exactly 1 hr slow. True EDT = logged time + 1 hr = 12:57, matching the tracker within 0.6 nm (vs. 9.6 nm off at the literal 11:57). Bermuda Race official timing is UTC−4 throughout — the instrument was simply on the wrong zone.

CONFIRMED — same UTC−5-instead-of-UTC−4 instrument error as the row above. True EDT = 17:21, matching the tracker within 0.7 nm (vs. 9.7 nm off at the literal 16:21). Gulf Stream entry was actually ~17:21, about an hour later than the log’s face value.
The word in the margin is "Kevin" — Steve's mid-ocean tribute to a friend and U.S. Coast Guard veteran who had recently passed away in a car accident.

meta.retireReason: Damaged rudder cable, all well onboard
